title: Coding Lab: Transaction Anomaly Scoring
description: Design a lightweight anomaly score for credit-card transactions using rolling statistics.
objectives: Maintain rolling mean and standard deviation per customer
Return a z-score style anomaly metric
Flag transactions whose score exceeds a configurable threshold
starterCode:
from collections import deque
import math

class RollingAnomalyScorer:
    def __init__(self, window=50):
        self.window = window
        self.values = {}  # customer_id -> deque

    def score(self, customer_id, amount, threshold=3.0):
        history = self.values.setdefault(customer_id, deque(maxlen=self.window))
        # TODO: compute rolling mean/std dev and return abs(z-score)
        history.append(amount)
        return 0.0
hints: Use Welford's online algorithm to update mean and variance in one pass
When there is not enough history, return 0 to avoid false alarms
Expose `window` and `threshold` so product managers can tune sensitivity
